mnemonics.title: Apache Features
mnemonics.content: M.A.P.A.C.H.E - Modular, Apache HTTP Server, Process-based, Cross-platform, High customization, Extensible with modules

mnemonics.title: Nginx Advantages
mnemonics.content: E.V.E.N.T - Event-driven, Very fast, Extremely low memory, Non-blocking, Tiny footprint

mnemonics.title: HTTP Status Codes
mnemonics.content: 1xx Info, 2xx Success, 3xx Redirect, 4xx Client Error, 5xx Server Error

acronyms.usage: Popular web development stack using Apache
Web development stack using Nginx instead of Apache
Protocol for executing programs to generate dynamic web content
Apache's architecture for handling concurrent connections
Improved version of CGI for better performance
Successor to SSL for secure communication

visualTips.visualization: Think of Apache as a restaurant with many waiters (processes/threads), each handling one table (request) at a time. Good for moderate traffic, but can get overwhelmed.
Think of Nginx as a single efficient manager handling many tables asynchronously, quickly passing orders to kitchen (backend) and serving when ready. Excellent for high concurrency.
Like a hotel receptionist (proxy) who receives guest requests and directs them to appropriate rooms/servers, without guests knowing which room they're in.
Like a traffic signal at a busy intersection, distributing cars (requests) across different roads (servers) to avoid congestion.
Like an apartment building (one physical server) with multiple apartments (websites), each with its own address (domain name).

analogies.analogy: The web server is like a restaurant: the host (server) takes orders (HTTP requests) from customers (browsers), kitchen (backend processing) prepares food (generates response), and host serves the prepared dish (response) to customers.
Network ports are like different phone lines at an office. Port 80 is the main line for regular web, Port 443 is the secure encrypted line for HTTPS, each handling specific types of communication.
.htaccess is like a sign on a hotel room door - it contains specific rules for that particular room (directory) without changing the entire hotel's (server's) main policies.
DocumentRoot is like the main entrance of a building - everything the public can access must come through or be located at this central point.

analogies.analogue: Apache modules are like items on a restaurant menu - you can add or remove them (enable/disable) to customize what services (features) are available.
